message:
fix(pages): fsync analytics/wizard JSON writes via write_file_durably helper [j7hqe]

Introduces a shared `write_file_durably()` helper in `src/pages/mod.rs` and
routes the five analytics bundle outputs plus the unencrypted-mode wizard
config.json through it so they survive a crash/power-loss between the write
and the OS-scheduled flush.

Problem:
  `std::fs::write` returns once the data is in the page cache; there is no
  guarantee the bytes have been persisted by the time control returns. If
  power is lost (or the filesystem is remounted) before the next periodic
  flush, the file can reappear as empty or missing — which for the pages
  wizard means a "successful" publish that silently lost its config.

Fix (non-Windows):
  - Open with create+truncate+write
  - `File::sync_all()` to fsync the file contents and metadata
  - fsync the parent directory so the dirent pointing at the new name is
    also durable (otherwise a crash can leave the bytes on disk but the
    name entry lost)

Windows:
  NTFS journals dirent updates synchronously and there is no portable
  directory-fsync on Windows, so the helper falls back to plain `fs::write`
  on that platform.

Call sites migrated:
  - AnalyticsBundle::write (statistics.json, timeline.json,
    workspace_summary.json, agent_summary.json, top_terms.json)
  - PagesWizard unencrypted-mode config.json

Mirrors the earlier sync_tree hardening landed under bead ...-92o31.
Unit tests in the new `tests` submodule prove (a) bytes round-trip and
(b) the helper surfaces the I/O error when the parent directory is
removed between the open and the fsync step.

Tracked in bead coding_agent_session_search-j7hqe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
